seoTitle: Torquay vs Torres Strait — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Torquay and Torres Strait. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Torquay vs Torres Strait: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Torquay and Torres Strait.
heading: Torquay vs Torres Strait — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Torquay, Queensland and Torres Strait, Queensland. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Torres Strait has a lower overall climate risk score (32/100) compared to Torquay (37/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Torquay or Torres Strait safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Torquay has a flood risk score of 32/100, while Torres Strait scores 33/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Torquay scores 50/100 for bushfire risk. Torres Strait scores 59/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Torquay or Torres Strait?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Torres Strait has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.98% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
